heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Stanley Kramer. Written by Abby Mann.
clean: Directed by Stanley Kramer. Written by Abby Mann.

heading: Col. Tad Lawson
raw: One thing about Americans: we're not cut out to be occupiers. We're new at it and not very good at it.
clean: we're not cut out to be occupiers. We're new at it and not very good at it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Capt. Harrison Byers: I trust you'll be comfortable in this room, sir.
clean: I trust you'll be comfortable in this room, sir.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judge Dan Haywood: Captain, I have no doubt that the entire state of Maine would be comfortable in this room!
clean: Captain, I have no doubt that the entire state of Maine would be comfortable in this room!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Werner Lampe: You do not think it was like that, do you? [the other defendants are silent] There were executions, yes, but nothing like that. Nothing at all! [turns to another prisoner] Pöhl. Pöhl! You ran those concentration camps, you and Eichmann. They say we killed millions of people. [scoffs] Millions of people! How could it be possible? Tell them, how could it be possible?
clean: You do not think it was like that, do you? There were executions, yes, but nothing like that. Nothing at all! Pöhl. Pöhl! You ran those concentration camps, you and Eichmann. They say we killed millions of people. Millions of people! How could it be possible? Tell them, how could it be possible?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ernst Janning: My counsel would have you believe that we were not aware of the extermination of millions. That we were only aware of the extermination of the hundreds. Does that make us any the less guilty? Maybe we didn't know the details. But if we didn't know, it was because we did not want to know.
clean: My counsel would have you believe that we were not aware of the extermination of millions. That we were only aware of the extermination of the hundreds. Does that make us any the less guilty? Maybe we didn't know the details. But if we didn't know, it was because we did not want to know.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judge Dan Haywood: Order, order! Put that man back in his seat and keep him there!
clean: Order, order! Put that man back in his seat and keep him there!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hans Rolfe: I'll make you a wager...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judge Dan Haywood: I don't make wagers.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Hans Rolfe: A gentleman's wager... in five years, the men you sentenced to life imprisonment will be free.
clean: A gentleman's wager... in five years, the men you sentenced to life imprisonment will be free.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Ernst Janning: Judge Haywood... the reason I asked you to come: Those people, those millions of people... I never knew it would come to that. You must believe it, you must believe it!
clean: Judge Haywood... the reason I asked you to come: Those people, those millions of people... I never knew it would come to that. You must believe it, you must believe it!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Judge Dan Haywood: Herr Janning, it "came to that" the first time you sentenced a man to death you knew to be innocent.
clean: Herr Janning, it "came to that" the first time you sentenced a man to death you knew to be innocent.
